**Alexander Nanta Linggi** (born 16 June 1958) is a Malaysian politician who served as the [Minister of Works](/wiki/Minister_of_Works_(Malaysia) "Minister of Works (Malaysia)") since 2022. A member of [Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu](/wiki/Parti_Pesaka_Bumiputera_Bersatu "Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu"), he represented [Kapit](/wiki/Kapit_(federal_constituency) "Kapit (federal constituency)") in the [Parliament of Malaysia](/wiki/Parliament_of_Malaysia "Parliament of Malaysia") since 1999.

He is Secretary-General of both [Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu](/wiki/Parti_Pesaka_Bumiputera_Bersatu "Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu") (PBB) and its coalition [Gabungan Parti Sarawak](/wiki/Gabungan_Parti_Sarawak "Gabungan Parti Sarawak") (GPS). He has served as [Minister of Works](/wiki/Minister_of_Works_(Malaysia) "Minister of Works (Malaysia)") in the Unity Government administration under Prime Minister [Anwar Ibrahim](/wiki/Anwar_Ibrahim "Anwar Ibrahim") since December 2022. He served as the [Minister of Domestic Trade and Consumer Affairs](/wiki/Minister_of_Domestic_Trade_and_Consumer_Affairs_(Malaysia) "Minister of Domestic Trade and Consumer Affairs (Malaysia)") for the second term in the [Barisan Nasional](/wiki/Barisan_Nasional "Barisan Nasional") (BN) administration under former Prime Minister [Ismail Sabri Yaakob](/wiki/Ismail_Sabri_Yaakob "Ismail Sabri Yaakob") from August 2021 to the collapse of the BN administration in November 2022 and his first term in [Perikatan Nasional](/wiki/Perikatan_Nasional "Perikatan Nasional") (PN) administration under former Prime Minister [Muhyiddin Yassin](/wiki/Muhyiddin_Yassin "Muhyiddin Yassin") from March 2020 to the collapse of the PN administration in August 2021 as well as the [Deputy Minister of Rural and Regional Development](/wiki/Minister_of_Rural_and_Regional_Development_(Malaysia) "Minister of Rural and Regional Development (Malaysia)") in the [Barisan Nasional](/wiki/Barisan_Nasional "Barisan Nasional") (BN) administration under former Prime Minister [Najib Razak](/wiki/Najib_Razak "Najib Razak") and former Ministers [Shafie Apdal](/wiki/Shafie_Apdal "Shafie Apdal") and [Ismail Sabri Yaakob](/wiki/Ismail_Sabri_Yaakob "Ismail Sabri Yaakob") from May 2013 to the collapse of the [BN](/wiki/Barisan_Nasional "Barisan Nasional") administration in May 2018. He has also served as the [Member of Parliament](/wiki/Dewan_Rakyat "Dewan Rakyat") (MP) for [Kapit](/wiki/Kapit_(federal_constituency) "Kapit (federal constituency)") since November 1999.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He is also the cousin of [Wilson Ugak Kumbong](/wiki/Wilson_Ugak_Kumbong "Wilson Ugak Kumbong"), the current [Deputy Minister of Digital](/wiki/Minister_of_Digital_(Malaysia) "Minister of Digital (Malaysia)") and MP for [Hulu Rajang](/wiki/Hulu_Rajang_(federal_constituency) "Hulu Rajang (federal constituency)").

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alexander_Nanta_Linggi&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Nanta is the grandson of former Sarawak leaders Tun [Jugah anak Barieng](/wiki/Jugah_anak_Barieng "Jugah anak Barieng") (patrilineal) and Temenggong Dato' Banyang anak Janting (matrilineal) and the son of former politician Tan Sri Leonard Linggi Jugah. In turn he is also the son-in-law of another politician, Tan Sri [Celestine Ujang Jilan](/wiki/Celestine_Ujang_Jilan "Celestine Ujang Jilan"), as he is married to the latter's daughter, Datin Angelina.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He is also a businessman, having served as the Chairman of shipping company Swee Joo Bhd.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Political career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alexander_Nanta_Linggi&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Political career")]

On 16 May 2013, after Nanta's victory in the [2013 Malaysian general election](/wiki/2013_Malaysian_general_election "2013 Malaysian general election"), he was appointed as Deputy Minister for Rural and Regional Development in the new [Cabinet](/wiki/Second_Najib_cabinet "Second Najib cabinet") of Prime Minister [Najib Razak](/wiki/Najib_Razak "Najib Razak").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Later in 2020, he became a full cabinet member by holding the position of [Minister of Domestic Trade and Consumer Affairs](/wiki/Minister_of_Domestic_Trade_and_Consumer_Affairs_(Malaysia) "Minister of Domestic Trade and Consumer Affairs (Malaysia)") after defending the Kapit parliamentary seat in the [2018 Malaysian general election](/wiki/2018_Malaysian_general_election "2018 Malaysian general election") for more than five terms (although it was the only election that witnessed a change in government after 61 years of dominant-party rule by the [Barisan Nasional](/wiki/Barisan_Nasional "Barisan Nasional")), in the aftermath of the [2020–2022 Malaysian political crisis](/wiki/2020%E2%80%932022_Malaysian_political_crisis "2020–2022 Malaysian political crisis") and held this ministerial position until the 2022 general elections.

In 2022, he was appointed as [Minister of Works](/wiki/Minister_of_Works_(Malaysia) "Minister of Works (Malaysia)") and has remained since.
